windows.embed_text_v2: Pouring white soy wax flakes from a clear plastic bag into a patterned ceramic bowl to measure wax, stainless steel bowl of wax flakes alongside on marble counter
windows.summary_v2: White candle wax flakes are poured from a clear plastic bag into a patterned ceramic bowl to portion out wax, beside a steel bowl already holding flakes.

seconds_v2.desc: A stainless steel bowl holding white wax flakes sits on a marble counter beside an empty patterned ceramic bowl.
A clear plastic bag of white wax flakes is lowered toward the empty patterned bowl on the right.
The bag tips and the first white wax flakes begin spilling into the patterned bowl.
Wax flakes pour steadily from the bag, a small mound forming in the bottom of the patterned bowl.
More flakes are poured in; the patterned bowl is filling with loose white wax flakes.
The pour continues, the heap of wax flakes in the patterned bowl growing larger.
The bag dispenses more flakes, nearly filling the patterned bowl to match the steel bowl's amount.
Pouring slows as the patterned bowl is now well filled with a rounded pile of wax flakes.
The bag tips for a final bit; both bowls now hold measured portions of white wax flakes on the marble counter.

windows.summary_v2: Golden wax melts in a stainless steel bowl set over a saucepan of water on a stovetop, a double-boiler used to melt candle wax.

seconds_v2.desc: A stainless steel bowl of melted golden wax sits inside a saucepan of water on a black cooktop, a double-boiler melting setup against a marble-tile backsplash.
The pale golden molten wax rests in the steel bowl over the simmering water bath, melting evenly.
The double boiler continues heating; the clear amber wax pool stays smooth in the bowl.
Melted wax in the steel bowl warms over the water-filled saucepan, surface glassy and still.
The wax remains fully liquid and golden in the double boiler on the stovetop.
The bowl of molten wax keeps heating gently over the water bath, no solids remaining.
Clear liquid wax sits in the steel bowl atop the saucepan, melting process ongoing.
The double-boiler arrangement holds the smooth pool of melted golden wax over heat.
The wax stays fully melted and amber-clear in the steel bowl over the water-filled pot.

windows.embed_text_v2: Centering and straightening two metal-tab wicks in an empty patterned ceramic bowl, holding wicks upright with bamboo skewers across the rim, marble counter
windows.summary_v2: A hand centers and straightens two metal-tabbed wicks in an empty patterned ceramic bowl, using bamboo skewers laid across the rim to hold them upright.

seconds_v2.desc: An empty patterned ceramic bowl holds two upright wicks on metal tabs, with wooden skewers laid across the rim to support them.
The two wicks stand at the bottom of the bowl, loosely held between pairs of bamboo skewers spanning the top.
A hand reaches in and pinches one wick, repositioning it upright between the skewers in the bowl.
The hand adjusts the skewers and wick, working to center the wicks in the empty bowl.
Fingers slide the bamboo skewers along the rim to clamp and straighten both wicks.
The hand withdraws; the two wicks now stand more upright, held centered by the crossed skewers.
Both wicks are positioned vertically between the skewers laid across the patterned bowl.
A closer angle shows the two metal-tabbed wicks centered and steadied by a bamboo skewer across the rim.
The wicks stand straight in the bowl, secured upright by the skewer resting on the bowl's edge.

tags_v2.action: pouring second wax layer around wicks

seconds_v2.desc: A patterned ceramic bowl holds a layer of pale yellow-green wax with two wicks standing upright, steadied by bamboo skewers laid across the rim.
The two wicks remain centered in the set yellow wax, held vertical between the bamboo skewers spanning the bowl.
The filled bowl of yellow wax sits on the marble counter, wicks upright between the skewers.
The wicks stay positioned in the pale wax layer, supported by the skewers across the top.
A glass measuring pitcher of clear pale wax appears at the upper left, approaching the wicked bowl.
The pitcher of liquid wax is brought closer to the bowl while the wicks stay held by the skewers.
A hand positions the measuring pitcher beside the bowl, preparing to add more wax over the set layer.
The pitcher hovers at the rim; the wicks remain centered in the yellow wax below.
The hand tips the glass pitcher and begins pouring clear liquid wax into the bowl around the two upright wicks.
